---
status: draft
shortDescription: "In stack and heap diagrams, an object on the heap is labeled with the name of the variable that refers to it"
concepts:
  - Object
  - Variable
  - Name
related:
- id: ObjectsMustBeNamed
  relation: Possible cause
inOtherPl:
# - pl: JavaScript
#   id: ObjectLabeledWithVariableName
# - pl: Python
#   id: ObjectLabeledWithVariableName
spec:
# - id: jls13
#   sections: ["11.8"]
plVersion:
# from: jls8
# to: jls14
specificToNm: StackHeapGlobalDiagram
expressibleInNms:
- id: StackHeapGlobalDiagram
  how: two variables referring to the same heap object
---
When drawing an object in the heap of a stack and heap diagram, the label of the object must be the name of the variable that points to that object (e.g., given code such as `Pacman pac = new Pacman()`, the object is labeled as `pac`).

frontmatter.shortDescription: In stack and heap diagrams, an object on the heap is labeled with the name of the variable that refers to it
